entities.name: GROUNDNUT

entities.description: A major fungal disease caused by Cercospora arachidicola that limits groundnut production

entities.name: RUST
entities.description: A major fungal disease caused by Puccinia arachidis that limits groundnut production

entities.description: The fungal pathogen responsible for causing early leaf spot

entities.name: PUCCINIA ARACHIDIS
entities.description: The fungal pathogen responsible for causing rust

entities.name: SENEGAL
entities.description: One of the locations where early leaf spot was evaluated

entities.description: One of the locations where early leaf spot was evaluated

entities.name: DHARWAD
entities.description: The location where rust was evaluated

entities.name: MODIFIED 9-POINT SCALE
entities.description: The scoring system used to evaluate the severity of early leaf spot and rust

entities.name: HIGH-DENSITY GENOTYPING
entities.description: The process of identifying genomic regions associated with disease resistance

entities.name: WHOLE GENOME RESEQUENCING
entities.description: The method used to generate high-density genotyping data

entities.name: SNPS
entities.description: Single nucleotide polymorphisms used as genetic markers for association studies

entities.name: GENOME-WIDE ASSOCIATION STUDY
entities.description: A method used to dissect the genetic architecture of resistance to fungal diseases

entities.name: MARKER TRAIT ASSOCIATIONS
entities.description: The statistical associations between genetic markers and phenotypic traits

entities.name: EARLY LEAF SPOT RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of groundnut to resist early leaf spot disease

entities.description: The ability of groundnut to resist rust disease

entities.description: A candidate gene associated with both rust and early leaf spot resistance

entities.name: BRASSINOSTEROIDS BIOSYNTHESIS
entities.description: A biological process involving sterol c4-methyl oxidase 1-2

entities.description: A breeding technique used to integrate favorable alleles into elite cultivars
